g.data-narration: Topological Sorting arranges graph vertices in a linear order respecting all directional dependencies.
g.text: A Fundamental Algorithm for Directed Acyclic Graphs

g.data-narration: A topological order ensures that for every directed edge from u to v, u appears before v in the sequence.
g.text: Arranges vertices of a DAG in linear order where all edges point forward
For edge (u → v): vertex u appears BEFORE vertex v

g.data-narration: Only Directed Acyclic Graphs can be topologically sorted. Cycles make it impossible due to circular dependencies.
Indegree counts incoming edges; zero-indegree vertices are sources that can start the ordering.
Consider this DAG with vertices 1-5 and directed edges showing dependencies between tasks.
Kahn's Algorithm uses BFS: calculate indegrees, queue sources, process and decrement neighbor indegrees.
Step-by-step: Start with source vertex 1, remove its edges, add new sources to queue when indegrees become zero.
DFS-based approach: visit all descendants first, add vertex to result after exploration, then reverse.
g.text: Key: After exploring ALL neighbors, add vertex to front of list. Finally reverse to get topological order.

g.data-narration: DFS visits: 1→2→4→5, then backtracks adding vertices in reverse: 5,4,2, then 3, finally 1.
If topological order contains fewer vertices than the graph, a cycle exists. This is key for cycle detection.
g.text: If the resulting topological order has fewer vertices than the input graph,
a CYCLE exists in the graph!

g.data-narration: Both algorithms have O(V+E) time and O(V) space complexity. Kahn's uses a queue while DFS uses recursion.
Topological sorting applies to build systems, task scheduling, package dependencies, and course prerequisites.
Key takeaways: Topological sort only works on DAGs, uses indegree zero vertices, and detects cycles.

g.text: • Only DAGs can be topologically sorted (no cycles allowed)
• Kahn's algorithm: process zero-indegree vertices, decrement neighbor indegrees
• DFS method: add vertex after exploring all descendants, then reverse
• Both have O(V+E) time and O(V) space complexity

g.data-narration: Exam tips: Always check for cycles first, remember to decrement indegrees, and reverse DFS results.

g.text: • Forgetting to decrement indegrees in Kahn's
• Forgetting to reverse result in DFS approach
• Multiple valid orders possible - any gets full marks

g.data-narration: Thank you for learning Topological Sorting. Practice with different graphs to master both algorithms.
g.text: Practice both Kahn's and DFS methods to master the topic!
